verses.text: Naomi seeks 'rest' — a settled home and security — for Ruth, and sends her to claim Boaz as kinsman-redeemer. The plan is bold, yet it rests on God's own law of redemption. To love another is often to work quietly for their good, trusting the LORD to crown the effort.

Naomi seeks 'rest' — a settled home and security — for Ruth, and sends her to claim Boaz as kinsman-redeemer. The plan is bold, yet it rests on God's own law of redemption. To love another is often to work quietly for their good, trusting the LORD to crown the effort.

Naomi seeks 'rest' — a settled home and security — for Ruth, and sends her to claim Boaz as kinsman-redeemer. The plan is bold, yet it rests on God's own law of redemption. To love another is often to work quietly for their good, trusting the LORD to crown the effort.

Naomi seeks 'rest' — a settled home and security — for Ruth, and sends her to claim Boaz as kinsman-redeemer. The plan is bold, yet it rests on God's own law of redemption. To love another is often to work quietly for their good, trusting the LORD to crown the effort.

Naomi seeks 'rest' — a settled home and security — for Ruth, and sends her to claim Boaz as kinsman-redeemer. The plan is bold, yet it rests on God's own law of redemption. To love another is often to work quietly for their good, trusting the LORD to crown the effort.

At his feet in the night Ruth makes her appeal: 'spread thy skirt over thine handmaid, for thou art a near kinsman.' She asks the redeemer to cover and claim her. So the soul comes to Christ — empty-handed, casting itself on the One with both the right and the power to redeem.

At his feet in the night Ruth makes her appeal: 'spread thy skirt over thine handmaid, for thou art a near kinsman.' She asks the redeemer to cover and claim her. So the soul comes to Christ — empty-handed, casting itself on the One with both the right and the power to redeem.

At his feet in the night Ruth makes her appeal: 'spread thy skirt over thine handmaid, for thou art a near kinsman.' She asks the redeemer to cover and claim her. So the soul comes to Christ — empty-handed, casting itself on the One with both the right and the power to redeem.

At his feet in the night Ruth makes her appeal: 'spread thy skirt over thine handmaid, for thou art a near kinsman.' She asks the redeemer to cover and claim her. So the soul comes to Christ — empty-handed, casting itself on the One with both the right and the power to redeem.

Boaz blesses her and promises to act — but only lawfully, honouring the nearer kinsman's prior right. Here is a man whose kindness never cuts corners. True integrity will not seize even a good thing by a wrong path, but trusts God to give what is rightly His to give.

Boaz blesses her and promises to act — but only lawfully, honouring the nearer kinsman's prior right. Here is a man whose kindness never cuts corners. True integrity will not seize even a good thing by a wrong path, but trusts God to give what is rightly His to give.

Ruth goes home with six measures of barley, a pledge of Boaz's intent, and Naomi counsels, 'sit still... the man will not be in rest until he have finished the thing.' Faith can wait quietly, for the Redeemer is more eager to finish our redemption than we are to receive it.
